title: Dog Loud Stomach Gurgling Noises: Causes and When to Worry
meta_desc: Is your dog's stomach gurgling loudly? Learn about borborygmi, what causes it, and when you should contact a veterinarian for help.
content_html:
What is it?

Loud stomach noises, medically known as borborygmi, occur when gas or fluids move through the digestive tract. Just like in humans, a dog's digestive system is constantly at work. These sounds can be louder when a dog is hungry, has swallowed excess air, or is processing a slightly upset stomach from a minor dietary change.

Is it an emergency?

The urgency level for loud stomach gurgling alone is Low. In most cases, these noises are a normal part of digestion and will resolve on their own. However, if the gurgling is accompanied by signs of distress—such as a bloated or hard abdomen, repeated vomiting, extreme lethargy, or a complete loss of appetite—the urgency level increases and you should seek veterinary care immediately.

Why a photo helps triage

Providing a photo or video can significantly assist in a remote triage. A photo of your dog’s posture (for instance, if they are 'stretching' frequently to relieve abdominal pain) or a photo of any abnormal stool can help a professional determine if the gurgling is benign or if it indicates a more serious gastrointestinal issue.

faqs.question: Should I feed my dog if their stomach is gurgling?
faqs.answer: If your dog is acting normally and has no vomiting or diarrhea, you can offer a small, bland meal like boiled chicken and white rice to see if it settles their stomach.

faqs.question: How long do these noises usually last?
faqs.answer: Normal digestive sounds usually come and go within a few hours. If the noises persist for more than 24 hours or are followed by diarrhea, consult your vet.

faqs.question: Can gas cause loud gurgling?
faqs.answer: Yes, excess gas moving through the intestines is one of the most common causes of loud borborygmi in dogs.
